join_order.notes: adsh_code == sub row_id (dict encoded as row index); load sub/sic.bin and sub/cik.bin into flat arrays; build sub_valid bitset[86135] marking rows where sic in [4000,4999]; O(1) probe per num row via adsh_code index

build tag_join_map: compact_hash_map keyed by uint64_t pack(tag_code:int32 << 32 | ver_code:int32) -> (int32_t row_id, int8_t abstract); build by iterating shared/tag_numpre.dict and shared/version_numpre.dict to construct string->code inverse maps, then iterating tag table rows to populate map; use tag_pk_hash to resolve tag+version strings to tag row_id during build; ~1.07M entries; ~20 MB

join_order.probe: num.(adsh_code, tag_code, ver_code) after sub+tag filters
join_order.notes: binary search pre_key_sorted for (adsh_code, tag_code, ver_code) using std::lower_bound / upper_bound on PreKeyEntry{adsh,tag,ver,row_id}; for each matching pre row_id check pre/stmt.bin[row_id] == eq_code; estimated ~700K qualifying num probes x O(log 9.6M)=23 comparisons = ~16M comparisons; pre sorted by (adsh,tag,ver) so range is typically 1 row

filters.method: num_zonemaps block skip (USD=code 0, leading blocks are all-USD contiguous); then per-row uom_code == usd_code check
sub_valid bitset probe on num.adsh_code; bitset fits in L1 (11 KB)
tag_join_map hash probe on packed(tag_code, ver_code); returns abstract flag inline; skip if abstract != 0
load eq_code from shared/stmt.dict at runtime; check pre/stmt.bin[pre_row_id] == eq_code after binary search hit

data_structures.sub_valid_bitset: bitset(86135) — marks adsh codes where sic in [4000,4999]; 11 KB; L1-resident during scan
data_structures.sub_sic_array: int16_t[86135] — direct lookup sic value by adsh_code for group key construction
data_structures.sub_cik_array: int32_t[86135] — direct lookup cik by adsh_code for COUNT(DISTINCT) accumulation
data_structures.tag_join_map: compact_hash_map<uint64_t, struct{int32_t row_id; int8_t abstract}> keyed by pack(tag_code<<32|ver_code); ~1.07M entries; robin-hood or flat open-addressing; ~20 MB
data_structures.tag_tlabel_offsets: int64_t[1070663] mmap from tag/tlabel.offsets — varlen tlabel offset array; accessed at output decode stage by tag_row_id
data_structures.tag_tlabel_data: mmap raw bytes from tag/tlabel.data — varlen tlabel content; read range [offsets[row_id], offsets[row_id+1])
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map<struct{int16_t sic; int32_t tag_row_id}, struct{double sum; int64_t cnt_val; int64_t cnt_rows; flat_hash_set<int32_t> cik_set}> per thread; thread-local to avoid contention; merged sequentially post-scan

parallelism.scan_unit: num block of 100,000 rows; 395 blocks total; dynamically dispatched to worker threads via atomic block counter
parallelism.merge_phase: sequential merge of thread-local aggregation maps after parallel scan; union cik_sets per group key

indexes.purpose: skip non-USD num blocks at scan entry; USD=code 0 so all-USD blocks are at the front; check uom_min==0 && uom_max==0 to fast-path entire block; mixed/non-USD blocks filtered per-row
build phase only: given tag+version string pair, resolve to tag table row_id; used when populating tag_join_map from tag table rows
inner join on composite key (adsh_code, tag_code, ver_code); mmap and binary search with std::lower_bound; comparison order: adsh -> tag -> ver
build phase: skip all-abstract tag blocks (abstract_min==1 && abstract_max==1) when iterating tag table to build tag_join_map; minor benefit since 96.4% rows have abstract=0

aggregation.key: struct{ int16_t sic; int32_t tag_row_id } (6 bytes; packed)
aggregation.accumulator: double sum_value, int64_t cnt_value (for AVG = sum/cnt), flat_hash_set<int32_t> cik_set (for COUNT DISTINCT)
aggregation.note: p.stmt is constant EQ for all surviving rows (predicate-filtered); stored as literal in output. tlabel decoded at final output stage from tag_tlabel arrays using tag_row_id from group key. AVG computed as sum_value / cnt_value at output.
